segments.text: I will bring yet one more plague on Pharaoh, and on Egypt; afterwards he will let you go. When he lets you go, he will surely thrust you out altogether.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Speak now in the ears of the people, and let every man ask of his neighbor, and every woman of her neighbor, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Yahweh gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Moreover, the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh’s servants, and in the sight of the people.
This is what Yahweh says: ‘About midnight I will go out into the middle of Egypt,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: and all the firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sits on his throne, even to the firstborn of the female servant who is behind the mill, and all the firstborn of livestock.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: There will be a great cry throughout all the land of Egypt, such as there has not been, nor will be any more.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: But against any of the children of Israel a dog won’t even bark or move its tongue, against man or animal, that you may know that Yahweh makes a distinction between the Egyptians and Israel.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: All these servants of yours will come down to me, and bow down themselves to me, saying, “Get out, with all the people who follow you;
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: and after that I will go out.’” He went out from Pharaoh in hot anger.
Pharaoh won’t listen to you, that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh, but Yahweh hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he didn’t let the children of Israel go out of his land.
